name: Transmontano Portuguese
notes: Transmontano Portuguese, spoken in Trás-os-Montes e Alto Douro (northeastern Portugal: Bragança, Miranda do Douro, Chaves, Alvarenga, Penafiel). Phonologically the most conservative and distinctive northern EP dialect: (1) most systematic retroflex sibilants of any EP dialect — /s/ and /z/ realised as [ʂ] and [ʐ] in nearly all environments including onset, coda, and intervocalic; (2) extreme unstressed vowel reduction — /ɐ/, /ə/ delete or devoice in rapid speech; (3) voiceless uvular coda [χ] alternating with [ʁ]; (4) conservative rhotic system: trill [r] retained in careful speech alongside flap [ɾ] — contrast with most EP varieties where [r] → [ʁ]; (5) Galician-Portuguese contact zone features near Miranda do Douro: /ʎ/ → [j] glide in some lexical items, Leonese loanwords; (6) archaic /e/ lowering to [ɛ] in pre-tonic syllables; (7) /u/ devoicing and deletion in unstressed post-tonic position; (8) no aspiration of stops; (9) prosodic pattern: stress-timed with extensive connected-speech reduction. Classified in Cintra's northern dialect zone with direct continuity into Galician; the Miranda do Douro micro-region also has Mirandese (a Leonese language) in contact, influencing phonological patterning of the Portuguese spoken there.

ancestors.notes: Standard European Portuguese; Transmontano diverges substantially in retroflex sibilants, conservative rhotics, and Galician-Leonese contact features

sources.author: Boléo, M.P.
sources.title: Estudos de linguística portuguesa e românica
